mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, markets price in imminent Federal Reserve tightening to combat inflation, causing a sharp repricing of equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses above the 235.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and expansion in total nonfarm payrolls signals robust labor market absorption, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production increases manufacturing output and supply chain throughput, which reduces input cost pressures and supports corporate margins, generating positive equity returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, with a negative or zero Rank IC in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in housing starts and retail sales indicate strong consumer balance sheet health and immediate spending capacity, providing a short-term earnings catalyst that pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where housing starts exceed 1.1 million units.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate revenue base for S&P 500 constituents, translating into higher quarterly earnings forecasts and positive equity returns over the subsequent three months.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no predictive power for 60-day forward returns, with a cumulative ICIR below 0.5 over the evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which compresses corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where the unemployment rate breaches the 5.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and housing construction reflects synchronized supply-side recovery, alleviating bottleneck risks and boosting short-term market sentiment for positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.
